response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but his background is centered on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), not embedded networking security. He fails multiple hard requirements: no Japanese proficiency (native-level required), no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and only brief, dated C work (six months at National Instruments in 2014-2015). There is no evidence of exposure to the core security and networking stack (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF/SNMP) or to LAN-switch firmware development.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Some low-level and performance-oriented work (C/C++ components for LabWindows/CVI, low-latency DLang services, robotics tooling)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and Linux/devops familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard native-level requirement for the role
No embedded LAN-switch, L2/L3 networking, or security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, tamper detection)
C for UNIX proficiency and Linux kernel modification experience are required but essentially absent from recent work

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
Broad senior engineering experience with some low-level exposure
